sentiment_score_definition: x <= -0.35: Bearish; -0.35 < x <= -0.15: Somewhat-Bearish; -0.15 < x < 0.15: Neutral; 0.15 <= x < 0.35: Somewhat_Bullish; x >= 0.35: Bullish
relevance_score_definition: 0 < x <= 1, with a higher score indicating higher relevance.

feed.title: The AES Corp Options Spot-On: On January 29th, 146.24K Contracts Were Traded, With 225.44K Open Interest
feed.summary: On January 29th, The AES Corp (AES.US) experienced active options trading, with a total volume of 146.24K contracts, overwhelmingly dominated by call options (98.64%). The open interest for AES reached approximately 225.44K contracts, representing 78.02% of the past 30-day average. A notable trade involved a call option with a $12.00 strike price expiring in February 2026, which recorded 28,190 contracts and a turnover of $9.02 million.

feed.title: Citizens Financial completes $400 million subordinated notes offering
feed.summary: Citizens Financial Group, Inc. has successfully completed a $400 million offering of 5.299% Fixed-Reset Subordinated Notes due 2036. The proceeds from this offering will be used for general corporate purposes, including share repurchases, dividends, and potential acquisitions, aiming to enhance the company's capital flexibility. TipRanks' AI Analyst, Spark, rates CFG as "Outperform" due to solid financial performance, positive earnings outlook, and planned buybacks.

feed.title: Abbott Laboratories Options Spot-On: On January 29th, 154.5K Contracts Were Traded, With 195.89K Open Interest
feed.summary: On January 29th, Abbott Laboratories (ABT.US) saw significant options trading activity, with 154.5K contracts traded, predominantly put options (92.89%). The total open interest for ABT options reached 195.89K contracts, exceeding its 30-day average by 100.38%. A notable transaction involved 14,340 put option contracts with a strike price of $125.00 expiring on February 20th, 2026, generating $27.96 million in turnover.

feed.title: American Tower appoints new chief accounting officer
feed.summary: American Tower (AMT) has announced the appointment of Paul Blanchett as Senior Vice President and Chief Accounting Officer, effective April 27, 2026. Blanchett, with extensive experience from GXO Logistics, XPO Logistics, Baker Hughes, General Electric, and PricewaterhouseCoopers, will succeed Robert J. Meyer, who will assist with the transition before his retirement. Blanchett's compensation package includes a base salary of $450,000, performance bonuses, signing/relocation bonuses, and significant time-vested equity awards.

feed.title: Colony Bankcorp reports fourth quarter net income of $7.8 million
feed.summary: Colony Bankcorp, Inc. reported a fourth-quarter net income of $7.8 million, or $0.42 per diluted share, for 2025, which is an increase from the $7.4 million reported in the same period of 2024. The company saw significant growth in total loans and deposits and improved its net interest margin. Additionally, Colony Bankcorp recently completed a merger with TC Bancshares, Inc., extended its stock repurchase program, and appointed a new Chief of Brand and Business Development.

feed.title: Perplexity Inks Microsoft AI Cloud Deal Amid Dispute With Amazon
feed.summary: Perplexity, an AI startup, has signed a three-year, $750 million deal with Microsoft to utilize its Azure cloud service, including its Foundry service for deploying AI models from OpenAI, Anthropic, and xAI. This agreement comes amidst an ongoing legal dispute between Perplexity and its former cloud partner, Amazon.com Inc.

feed.title: Assurant declares quarterly dividend of $0.88 per share
feed.summary: Assurant, Inc. has announced a quarterly dividend of $0.88 per share, payable on March 30, 2026, to stockholders of record as of February 17, 2026. The company has a 22-year history of consecutive dividend increases and recently authorized a new $700 million share repurchase program while raising its quarterly dividend by 10%. Analysts have shown confidence in Assurant's future performance, with increased price targets citing strong segment growth.

feed.title: Arthur J. Gallagher & Co. (NYSE:AJG) Issues Quarterly Earnings Results, Misses Expectations By $0.04 EPS
feed.summary: Arthur J. Gallagher & Co. (NYSE:AJG) reported quarterly earnings of $2.38 EPS, missing the consensus estimate of $2.42, and revenue of $3.57 billion, falling short of the $3.61 billion expectation. Despite the earnings miss, the company announced an increase in its quarterly dividend to $0.70 per share, yielding 1.1%, and maintains a consensus "Hold" rating from analysts with a target price of $290.50. Insider transactions show recent sales by CFO Douglas K. Howell and General Counsel Walter D. Bay.

feed.title: Weyerhaeuser (NYSE:WY) Issues Quarterly Earnings Results, Beats Expectations By $0.04 EPS
feed.summary: Weyerhaeuser (NYSE:WY) announced its quarterly earnings, reporting an EPS of ($0.09), which surpassed the consensus estimate of ($0.13) by $0.04. Despite beating earnings expectations, revenue came in slightly below estimates at $1.54 billion versus $1.58 billion. The company also declared a quarterly dividend of $0.21, although its payout ratio of 186.67% suggests the dividend exceeds current earnings.

feed.title: Stryker (NYSE:SYK) Releases Earnings Results, Beats Expectations By $0.08 EPS
feed.summary: Stryker (NYSE:SYK) announced its quarterly earnings, reporting $4.47 EPS and $7.17 billion in revenue, both exceeding analyst expectations. Following the report, shares dropped slightly to $354.39, despite the company also raising its quarterly dividend to $0.88 per share. Analysts maintain a "Moderate Buy" rating with an average target price of $425.94, while insiders have sold a significant number of shares recently.

feed.title: ResMed earnings beat by $0.08, revenue was in line with estimates
feed.summary: ResMed (NYSE: RMD) reported a strong second quarter, with EPS of $2.81, exceeding analyst estimates by $0.08. The company's revenue for the quarter was $1.4 billion, aligning with consensus estimates. ResMed's stock has seen positive movement over the last three months, and its financial health is rated as "great performance" by InvestingPro.

feed.title: Vistra (VST) Valuation Check After PJM Regulatory Concerns And Long Term Meta Nuclear Deal
feed.summary: Vistra (VST) stock is under scrutiny following new analyst commentary regarding increased regulatory risk from potential PJM market interventions, even as the company secured a 20-year nuclear power purchase deal with Meta. Despite a significant 3-year return, the market narrative suggests Vistra is 29% undervalued at $233 per share, largely due to anticipated long-term electricity demand growth driven by AI and data centers. However, its high P/E ratio and reliance on fossil assets present risks that could challenge this growth story.

feed.title: Brand Engagement Network (BNAI) Is Up 624.1% After Exclusive African AI Licensing Partnership Announcement
feed.summary: Brand Engagement Network (BNAI) has seen a massive stock price increase following its announcement of an exclusive AI licensing partnership with Valio Technologies for African markets. This collaboration includes a student wellbeing pilot at Nelson Nelson Nelson University, using BNAI's Engagement Language Model and Retrieval-Augmented Generation technologies. Despite this positive development, the company is still considered an early-stage, high-risk AI platform with minimal revenue and significant losses, meaning financial impact from this partnership may take time to materialize.

feed.title: Eastman Chemical falls as Q1 guidance disappoints
feed.summary: Eastman Chemical (NYSE: EMN) shares fell after the company reported in-line Q4 earnings but missed revenue expectations and provided Q1 2026 guidance below analyst estimates. For Q1 2026, Eastman expects adjusted EPS between $1.00 and $1.20, lower than the analyst consensus of $1.28. Despite challenges, the company plans to increase cost reduction actions and expects a "substantial sequential increase" in adjusted EPS for the first quarter of 2026.

feed.title: Bright Horizons Family Solutions Announces Date of Fourth Quarter 2025 Earnings Release and Conference Call
feed.summary: Bright Horizons Family Solutions Inc. (NYSE: BFAM) will release its Q4 2025 earnings after the market closes on Thursday, February 12, 2026. Following the release, the company will host a conference call at 5:00 p.m. ET to discuss its results, business outlook, and strategy. Interested parties can access the earnings release and webcast link via the investor relations section of the company's website.

feed.title: Arq Signs Credit Agreement Amendment With MidCap Funding
feed.summary: Arq Inc. has signed a Third Amendment to its revolving Credit, Security and Guaranty Agreement with MidCap Funding. This amendment extends changes to borrowing availability and temporarily reduces minimum liquidity requirements, maintaining a $2.0 million threshold until February 27, 2026, after which it increases to $5.0 million. The changes are intended to provide near-term financial flexibility for the company.

feed.title: Deckers Outdoor achieves record revenue and profits, raises FY26 outlook (DECK:NYSE)
feed.summary: Deckers Outdoor (DECK) reported record revenue and profits in its fiscal third quarter, driven by strong demand for UGG and HOKA footwear across both wholesale and direct-to-consumer channels. The company raised its financial outlook for FY26 above Wall Street expectations, forecasting higher profit, sales, and margins. Despite rising costs impacting profit margins, the robust performance of its key brands propelled significant growth.

feed.title: KLA Corporation drops after Q2 beat and mixed outlook
feed.summary: KLA Corporation (NASDAQ: KLAC) shares dropped about 7% in extended trading after the company reported a better-than-expected second quarter but provided a mixed outlook for the third quarter. Despite the strong Q2 performance and record calendar year 2025 results driven by increasing demand for process control tools at advanced semiconductor nodes and AI-related infrastructure investments, its Q3 earnings forecast bracketed Wall Street estimates, and revenue guidance was also mixed. The semiconductor equipment maker continues to benefit from long-term trends in AI, foundry, logic chips, memory, and advanced packaging.

feed.title: Atlantic Union Bankshares Declares Quarterly Common and Preferred Dividends
feed.summary: Atlantic Union Bankshares (AUB) announced a quarterly common stock dividend of $0.37 per share, consistent with the previous quarter but an 8.8% increase year-on-year, yielding approximately 3.8%. The company also declared a quarterly dividend of $0.43 per depositary share for its Series A preferred stock, reinforcing its commitment to returning capital to shareholders. TipRanks' AI Analyst, Spark, rates AUB as "Outperform" citing strong financial performance and a constructive earnings outlook.

feed.title: News | Blackstone reports record results, powered by data center investments
feed.summary: Blackstone, the world's largest commercial property owner, reported record earnings driven by strong gains from investment sales and its strategic focus on artificial intelligence-related infrastructure, particularly data centers. The firm's assets under management reached a record $1.3 trillion, with significant capital flowing into its real estate segments, especially core-plus investments. Despite some headwinds in other sectors, Blackstone's leadership is optimistic about a recovering real estate market, continuing to invest heavily in data centers and digital infrastructure.

feed.title: Justin Moore, Author at Unit 42
feed.summary: This page introduces Justin Moore, a Senior Manager at Unit 42, specializing in threat intelligence, nation-state attribution, and technical analysis. It highlights his extensive background, including previous roles at TikTok and Mandiant, as well as his experience with special mission units. The page serves as an author profile, directing users to more content by Justin Moore and offering subscriptions to Unit 42 threat research.

feed.title: Jefferies Raises Price Target for Danaher (DHR) to $265, Maintai
feed.summary: Jefferies has increased its price target for Danaher (DHR) to $265 from $255, while maintaining a Buy rating, signaling continued confidence in the company. This adjustment follows several other analysts who have also raised their price targets and reiterated Buy ratings for the stock. Danaher, a diversified company focused on life sciences and diagnostics, shows a positive outlook from Wall Street analysts with an average target price of $263.15, implying an upside of 19.82%.

feed.title: Sempra's Oncor Files for Rate Review Settlement Approval
feed.summary: Sempra's Oncor has filed for approval of a comprehensive rate review settlement. The proposed settlement includes a $6.975 billion annual revenue requirement, an 8.8% revenue increase adding $560 million annually, and a revised capital structure of 56.5% debt and 43.5% equity. A decision from the PUCT is anticipated in the first half of 2026.

feed.title: Slide to Report 2025 Fourth Quarter and Full Year Earnings Results on February 24, 2026
feed.summary: Slide Insurance Holdings, Inc. (Nasdaq: SLDE) announced it will release its 2025 fourth quarter and full year financial results on Tuesday, February 24, 2026, after the market closes. The company will also host a conference call and live webcast on Wednesday, February 25, 2026, at 8:30 AM Eastern time to discuss the results. Details for the webcast and dial-in are provided for interested investors.

feed.title: Halio Carey, global treasurer at Goldman Sachs, sells $2.6m in GS stock
feed.summary: Halio Carey, Global Treasurer at Goldman Sachs (NYSE: GS), sold 2,836 shares of the company's common stock for a total of $2,638,716 on January 27, 2026. Following this transaction, Carey directly owns 2,017 shares. The article also mentions other recent Goldman Sachs-related news, including its involvement in potential IPOs for Entrata and RAC, JPMorgan Chase's push for more M&A business, and the significant increase in CEO David Solomon's 2025 compensation.

feed.title: Insider Sale: EVP & CAO of $CSX Sells 66,667 Shares
feed.summary: Diana B Sorfleet, EVP & CAO of CSX ($CSX), sold 66,667 shares of the company stock on January 28, 2026, for an estimated $2,498,679, reducing her holdings of that class of stock by 28.1%. This sale is part of a trend of 3 insider sales and 1 insider purchase at CSX in the last six months. The article also details recent hedge fund activity, Q4 2025 revenue, congressional stock trading, and current analyst ratings and price targets for $CSX.

feed.title: Weyerhaeuser earnings beat by $0.04, revenue fell short of estimates
feed.summary: Weyerhaeuser (NYSE: WY) reported fourth-quarter EPS of $-0.09, surpassing analyst estimates by $0.04, although revenue of $1.54 billion fell short of the $1.57 billion consensus. The company's stock has seen a 12.74% rise in the last three months but is down 16.25% over the past year. InvestingPro rates Weyerhaeuser's financial health as "fair performance."

feed.title: Stryker shares higher as fourth quarter results beat expectations
feed.summary: Stryker Corporation reported better-than-expected fourth-quarter results, with adjusted earnings per share of $4.47 and revenue climbing 11.4% to $7.2 billion, leading to a 4% rise in shares during after-hours trading. The medical technology company achieved double-digit growth in both sales and adjusted earnings for the quarter and full year 2025, surpassing $25 billion in annual revenue for the first time. Stryker also provided an optimistic outlook for 2026, projecting organic sales growth between 8.0% and 9.5% and adjusted earnings per share of $14.90 to $15.10.

feed.title: Western Digital Q2 revenue increases 25% as data center demand drives growth
feed.summary: Western Digital (WDC) reported its Q2 2026 financial results, with revenue increasing by 25%, driven by robust data center demand. While results surpassed market expectations, shares experienced a slight dip in early post-market trading. The hard disk drive company released its adjusted earnings for the quarter ending December 31.

feed.title: State Street to Speak at the BofA Securities Financial Services Conference
feed.summary: State Street Corporation announced that its Chief Financial Officer, John Woods, will present at the BofA Securities Financial Services Conference on Tuesday, February 10, 2026, at 1:50 pm ET. An audio webcast and recorded replay of the event will be available on State Street’s Investor Relations website. State Street is a leading provider of financial services to institutional investors, with significant assets under custody and management globally.

feed.title: Nordson Corporation Announces Earnings Release and Webcast for First Quarter Fiscal Year 2026
feed.summary: Nordson Corporation (Nasdaq: NDSN) announced it will release its first-quarter fiscal year 2026 earnings on February 18, 2026, after the market close. The company will host a webcast the following day, February 19, 2026, at 8:30 AM ET for investors. A replay of the webcast will be available on Nordson's investor website for those unable to attend live.

feed.title: MarketAxess to Participate in the UBS Financial Services Conference
feed.summary: MarketAxess Holdings Inc. announced that its Chief Financial Officer, Ilene Fiszel Bieler, will participate in a fireside chat at the UBS Financial Services Conference on February 9, 2026, at 1:50 p.m. ET. A live webcast and replay of the chat will be available on the MarketAxess Investor Relations website.

feed.title: Amazon in talks to invest up to $50 billion in OpenAI, WSJ says
feed.summary: Amazon.com Inc. is reportedly in discussions to invest up to $50 billion in OpenAI, according to a Wall Street Journal report. This potential deal would strengthen ties between the e-commerce giant's cloud-computing arm and the leading AI tool developer. OpenAI is said to be seeking up to $100 billion in new capital from various investors, with Amazon CEO Andy Jassy and OpenAI CEO Sam Altman leading negotiations.

feed.title: AM Best Affirms Credit Ratings of Assurant Daños México, S.A. and Assurant Vida México, S.A.
feed.summary: AM Best has affirmed the Financial Strength Rating of A (Excellent) and Long-Term Issuer Credit Ratings of "a" (Excellent) for Assurant Daños México, S.A. (ADM) and Assurant Vida México, S.A. (AVM). The outlook for these ratings is stable, reflecting the companies' very strong balance sheet strength, adequate operating performance, and strategic importance within the Assurant Enterprise. The ratings also consider the solid reinsurance structure and ongoing parental support for growth in the Latin American market.

feed.title: Zacks Value Investor Highlights: The Sherwin-Williams, Berkshire, Bank of America
feed.summary: The Zacks Value Investor podcast, hosted by Tracey Ryniec, recently discussed lessons from long-term investors, including a 95-year-old individual named Ed and Warren Buffett. The episode highlights stocks like Sherwin-Williams, Bank of America, and Berkshire Hathaway, emphasizing the benefits of patience, discipline, and compounding in investing. It serves as a tribute to Ed's investing legacy and offers insights for current investors.

feed.title: Las Vegas Sands forecast to exceed US$5 billion in EBITDA for first time post-COVID this year on Macau growth, Singapore strength
feed.summary: Las Vegas Sands (LVS) is projected to exceed US$5 billion in group-wide EBITDA this year, a first since 2019, driven by strong performance at Marina Bay Sands (MBS) in Singapore and improved conditions in Macau. Despite mixed Q4 2025 results, analysts maintain a positive outlook, citing strategic management changes in Macau and MBS's continued dominance in the luxury market. Both Seaport Research Partners and CBRE have issued "Buy" ratings for LVS and Sands China shares.

feed.title: KLA: Fiscal Q2 Earnings Snapshot
feed.summary: KLA Corporation (KLAC) reported robust fiscal second-quarter earnings, with adjusted profits of $8.85 per share and revenues of $3.3 billion, both exceeding Wall Street expectations. The semiconductor equipment manufacturer projects strong financial performance for the upcoming fiscal third quarter as well.

feed.title: Gartner appoints AI expert Rus and investment veteran Bousa to board By Investing.com
feed.summary: Gartner, Inc. (NYSE:IT) has expanded its Board of Directors by appointing AI and robotics expert Daniela Rus and investment veteran Edward Bousa, increasing the board to 13 members. This move comes as the company's stock is trading near 52-week lows, despite reporting $6.46 billion in revenue and a 68% gross profit margin over the last twelve months. Analysts have mixed views on Gartner's future, with concerns about AI disruptions tempered by the company's durable business model.

feed.title: Hologic: Fiscal Q1 Earnings Snapshot
feed.summary: Hologic Inc. reported fiscal first-quarter earnings of $179.1 million, or 79 cents per share, which adjusted to $1.04 per share. This performance missed Wall Street's expectations of $1.09 per share and a revenue forecast of $1.07 billion, as the company posted $1.05 billion in revenue.

feed.title: GE Vernova Inc. SEC 10-K Report
feed.summary: GE Vernova Inc. has released its 2025 10-K report, detailing significant financial growth with total revenues of $38,068 million and a net income of $4,879 million. The report highlights strategic advancements, including its separation from General Electric, the acquisition of Prolec GE, and substantial investments in R&D and production capacity to meet global sustainability goals. The company also addresses operational, regulatory, and market risks while outlining management strategies to mitigate these challenges.

feed.title: Eastman Chemical Q4 sales fall on lower volumes
feed.summary: Eastman Chemical Company reported a decline in its fourth-quarter sales due to lower volumes. The financial news was disseminated via Reuters and highlighted by TradingView News. This brief report indicates a challenging period for the company's sales performance.

feed.title: Deckers: Fiscal Q3 Earnings Snapshot
feed.summary: Deckers Outdoor Corp. (DECK) reported strong fiscal third-quarter earnings, with net income reaching $481.1 million and a profit of $3.33 per share, significantly surpassing analyst expectations of $2.77 per share. The company also beat revenue forecasts, posting $1.96 billion against an expected $1.88 billion. Looking ahead, Deckers projects full-year earnings between $6.80 and $6.85 per share and revenue in the range of $5.4 billion to $5.43 billion.

feed.title: APPLIED MATERIALS INC /DE ($AMAT) President and CEO 2025 Pay Revealed
feed.summary: APPLIED MATERIALS INC /DE ($AMAT) CEO Gary E. Dickerson's estimated compensation for 2025 is $29,649,352, a 6.24% increase from 2024. The article also details recent insider trading activity, congressional stock trading, hedge fund activity, analyst ratings, and price targets for AMAT stock.

feed.title: CF Industries Holdings Just Shocked Wall Street – But Is This Stock Actually Worth Your Money?
feed.summary: CF Industries Holdings, a major nitrogen fertilizer producer, is drawing attention from investors despite not being a viral trend. The article explores whether the stock is a valuable investment or a risk, emphasizing its cyclical nature tied to natural gas prices and global food demand. It concludes that CF Industries is more suited for long-term, risk-aware investors who understand commodity cycles, rather than short-term traders.

feed.title: The Bancorp Reports 4Q 2025 EPS of $1.28, ROA of 2.53% and ROE of 30.4% Driven by NIM of 4.30%, Continued Fintech Fee Growth, and $150 Million in Share Repurchases in the Quarter
feed.summary: The Bancorp reported strong financial results for the fourth quarter of 2025, with diluted EPS of $1.28, a return on assets of 2.53%, and return on equity of 30.4%. The company executed $150 million in share repurchases, reducing outstanding shares by 5%. Despite falling short of some internal expectations due to factors like a government shutdown and legal settlement costs, The Bancorp saw continued growth in fintech fees and initiated optimistic EPS guidance for 2026 and 2027.

feed.title: ResMed: Fiscal Q2 Earnings Snapshot
feed.summary: ResMed Inc. reported strong fiscal second-quarter results, with net income reaching $392.6 million, or $2.81 per share when adjusted for one-time factors, surpassing Wall Street expectations. The company also exceeded revenue forecasts, posting $1.42 billion for the period. These figures demonstrate ResMed's solid financial performance, outperforming analyst estimates for both earnings and revenue.

feed.title: Reliance Global Group closes $2 million public offering
feed.summary: Reliance Global Group (NASDAQ:EZRA) has successfully closed a public offering, raising approximately $2 million in gross proceeds. The InsurTech company sold over 7.4 million shares (or pre-funded warrants) and warrants for an additional 14.8 million shares at $0.27 per share. The proceeds are earmarked for working capital, merger and acquisition strategies, and general corporate purposes, as the company continues to focus on technology sector investments and expand through strategic acquisitions.

feed.title: Kioxia and Sandisk Extend Yokkaichi Joint Venture Agreement Through 2034
feed.summary: Kioxia and Sandisk have extended their joint venture agreements for Kioxia’s Yokkaichi Plant through December 31, 2034, adding five years to the original agreement. This extension reinforces their 25-year partnership, focusing on advanced 3D flash memory production using AI-enabled manufacturing and economies of scale. Sandisk will pay Kioxia $1.165 billion for manufacturing services and supply availability, recognizing the value and profitability of Kioxia’s operations.

feed.title: Charles Schwab Bank CEO Paul Woolway to retire
feed.summary: Paul Woolway, CEO of Charles Schwab Bank, is set to retire from his position on July 1 after more than 16 years with the company. Charles Schwab announced the retirement on Thursday.

feed.title: Hologic misses Q1 earnings and revenue estimates, shares edge lower By Investing.com
feed.summary: Hologic, Inc. reported fiscal first-quarter earnings and revenue that missed analyst expectations, with revenue reaching $1.05 billion against an estimated $1.07 billion and adjusted EPS of $1.04 falling short of the $1.09 forecast. Following these mixed results, the company's stock experienced a slight dip. Hologic stated it is not providing financial guidance for fiscal 2026 due to its pending acquisition by Blackstone and TPG, with a stockholder meeting scheduled for February 5, 2026, to vote on the merger.
